data.name: About.Description
data.value: Desktop satellite tracking for amateur radio operators. Track passes, Doppler-corrected CAT control, rotator tracking, and transponder modes from the OscarWatch satellite database.

data.name: About.DonateNote
data.value: If you donate, please let Peter know your callsign so you can be thanked.

data.name: About.License
data.value: Licensed under the GNU Affero General Public License v3.0 or later (AGPL-3.0-or-later). See LICENSE.md in the source repository.

data.name: About.SupportIntro
data.value: OscarWatch is free and open source. If you find it useful, you can support ongoing development:

data.name: Main.Gps.TimeTooltip.Inactive
data.value: GPS time enabled — waiting for fix

data.name: Main.HamsAtRoves.Empty
data.value: No workable roves for your hams.at location.

data.name: Main.HamsAtRoves.LoadFailed
data.value: Could not load roves from hams.at.

data.name: Main.HamsAtRoves.ResizeGrip
data.value: Drag to resize the roves list

data.name: ComPort.DualRadioSamePort
data.value: Downlink and uplink radios both use {0}. Use different COM ports for each radio.

data.name: ComPort.RotatorAndDownlink
data.value: Rotator and downlink radio both use {0}. Use different COM ports or disable one device.

data.name: ComPort.RotatorAndUplink
data.value: Rotator and uplink radio both use {0}. Use different COM ports or disable one device.

data.name: ComPort.RotatorAndRadio
data.value: Rotator and radio both use {0}. Use different COM ports or disable one device.

data.name: ComPort.GpsAndRotator
data.value: GPS and rotator both use {0}. Use different COM ports or disable one device.

data.name: ComPort.GpsAndRadio
data.value: GPS and radio both use {0}. Use different COM ports or disable one device.

data.name: ComPort.GpsAndDownlink
data.value: GPS and downlink radio both use {0}. Use different COM ports or disable one device.

data.name: ComPort.GpsAndUplink
data.value: GPS and uplink radio both use {0}. Use different COM ports or disable one device.

data.name: A11y.Plot.SelectWithArrows
data.value: {0}. {1} satellite(s). Use arrow keys to select, Enter to confirm.

data.name: A11y.Plot.FocusedWithLookAngles
data.value: {0}. Focused {1}. Azimuth {2:F1} degrees, elevation {3:F1} degrees, altitude {4:F0} kilometres.

data.name: A11y.SkyPlot.TabHint
data.value: Sky plot. Tab here, then use arrow keys to change the selected satellite.

data.name: Main.Radio.CatPauseTip
data.value: Stop sending frequency updates to the radio so you can tune manually.

data.name: Main.SkyPlot.Hint
data.value: Click a satellite on the map or sky plot, or use arrow keys when focused.

data.name: Main.Standby.Hint
data.value: Browsing only — rotator parked, CAT paused

data.name: File.Import.Settings.ConfirmMessage
data.value:
Import replaces all current settings with the chosen file. COM ports, audio devices, and other machine-specific values may need adjustment on this PC. API keys in the file are restored as stored.
Continue?

data.name: Settings.FootprintArrows.Note
data.value: Draws a small arrow inside each satellite footprint on the world map showing ground-track direction.

data.name: Settings.GreylineOverlay.Note
data.value: Shades the night side of Earth and draws the terminator. Satellite footprints stay on top. Follows map time when you scrub.

data.name: Settings.Language.RestartNote
data.value: Language applies after you restart OscarWatch.

data.name: Settings.ClockFormat.Note
data.value: Applies to pass lists, planners, map clock, and other times in the app. UTC vs local is still chosen per dialogue.

data.name: Settings.Theme.Note
data.value: System follows your OS light/dark setting. The world map image stays light in all modes for now.

data.name: Status.HelpMissing
data.value: Help files not found — reinstall or run from a published build

data.name: Status.NoTle
data.value: No TLE data — check Settings → TLE or use Satellites → Refresh TLEs

data.name: Status.TransponderUpToDate
data.value: Transponder database is up to date.

data.name: Rig.SelectDualComPorts
data.value: Select COM ports for downlink and uplink radios

data.name: Dx.Validation.MinChars
data.value: Enter at least 4 characters (e.g. JO22 or JO22lk).

data.name: Freq.SelectSatelliteHint
data.value: Select a satellite on the map or from upcoming passes.

data.name: Freq.NoTransponder
data.value: No transponder data for this satellite

data.name: Freq.DopplerTxFixedToolTip
data.value: TX fixed — downlink Doppler only (SatPC32 TX-Fixed). Uplink stays on your CQ spot.

data.name: Freq.DopplerRxFixedToolTip
data.value: RX fixed — uplink Doppler only (SatPC32 RX-Fixed). Downlink stays put.

data.name: Freq.OffsetOnDownlinkTuneUplink
data.value: {0} on downlink. Tune Main for uplink.

data.name: Freq.DownlinkOnlyTuneUplink
data.value: Downlink only. Tune Main for uplink.

data.name: Freq.DopplerFullHint
data.value: Full — uplink and downlink both track Doppler.

data.name: Freq.DopplerTxFixedHint
data.value: TX fixed — uplink stays put; downlink tracks Doppler (roving CQ).

data.name: Freq.DopplerRxFixedHint
data.value: RX fixed — downlink stays put; uplink tracks Doppler.

data.name: Freq.OperatingCwSideband
data.value: CW on uplink; receive stays USB/LSB. Ctrl+W to toggle.

data.name: Freq.OperatingCwBoth
data.value: CW on uplink and downlink. Ctrl+W to toggle.

data.name: Freq.OperatingVoice
data.value: Voice uses database modes. Ctrl+W toggles CW.

data.name: Settings.Gps.Intro
data.value: Serial NMEA GPS or network gpsd for station position (lat/lon, grid square, altitude) and optional UTC for tracking.

data.name: Settings.Gps.GpsdNote
data.value: Connect to a running gpsd daemon (default port 2947). Typical on Linux or Raspberry Pi when the GPS is already shared on the network.

data.name: Settings.Gps.AutoUpdateStation
data.value: Automatically update station position from GPS fix

data.name: Settings.Gps.UseTimeForTracking
data.value: Use GPS UTC for satellite tracking

data.name: Settings.Gps.UseTimeNote
data.value: Uses GPS time for orbit propagation only — does not change the Windows system clock.

data.name: Settings.Gps.StatusNoPort
data.value: Select a COM port for the GPS.

data.name: Settings.Gps.StatusNoGpsdHost
data.value: Enter the gpsd host name or IP address.

data.name: Settings.Tle.SourceNote
data.value: OscarWatch is the default AMSAT-oriented catalog. AMSAT.org uses the published nasabare.txt set. Use a custom URL or local file if you maintain your own TLE set.

data.name: Settings.Tle.UpdatesNote
data.value: Automatic download applies to OscarWatch and custom URLs only. Local files are re-read when you save settings or choose Satellites → Refresh TLEs.

data.name: Settings.Tracking.PassWindowNote
data.value: Used for the upcoming pass list and as defaults in the pass planner.

data.name: Settings.Tracking.TransponderCheck
data.value: Check for transponder database updates on startup

data.name: Settings.Tracking.TransponderCheckNote
data.value: Downloads tle.oscarwatch.org/satellite_database.json when updates are available. New satellites and modes are offered for merge; your local edits are kept unless you accept published changes.

data.name: Settings.AppUpdate.Enabled
data.value: Check for application updates on startup and daily while running

data.name: Settings.AppUpdate.EnabledNote
data.value: Compares your version with the latest release on GitHub. You can also check manually from Help.

data.name: Settings.Voice.EnableNote
data.value: Announces each enabled satellite once per pass when it crosses the elevation threshold while rising, for example: Alpha Oscar Zero Seven is rising.

data.name: Settings.Voice.AnnounceElevationNote
data.value: Default is -3°. The announcement fires when elevation rises through this value (negative values catch the satellite before it clears the horizon).

data.name: Settings.Voice.SpeechUnavailable
data.value: Text-to-speech is not available on this system.

data.name: Settings.Radio.DualEnable
data.value: Dual radio (separate downlink and uplink — FT-817/818, FT-991(A), IC-705, IC-706 series, or mixed)

data.name: Settings.Radio.DualIncompleteBoth
data.value: Select COM ports for both downlink and uplink radios.

data.name: Settings.Radio.DualIncompleteDownlink
data.value: Select a COM port for the downlink radio.

data.name: Settings.Radio.DualIncompleteUplink
data.value: Select a COM port for the uplink radio.

data.name: Settings.Radio.CivHint
data.value: IC-9700 default A2, IC-910 default 7C (hex). Use 60 if your radio is set that way in the CI-V menu.

data.name: Settings.Radio.Ft847Hint
data.value: FT-847: set CAT baud in radio menu #37 (typically 57600). Use the CAT/LINEAR jack with a level converter (e.g. CT-62).

data.name: Settings.Radio.Ts2000Hint
data.value: TS-2000: CAT 57600 8N1. Select SAT on the radio and turn memory mode off before tracking; OscarWatch then enables SATL via CAT (SA/FA/FB layout), auto VHF/UHF band swap.

data.name: Settings.Radio.RegionNote
data.value: EU uses tone; USA uses tone squelch (subtone).

data.name: Settings.Radio.CatDelayNote
data.value: Minimum time between CAT commands to protect the serial link.

data.name: Settings.Radio.Ic705CivHint
data.value: IC-705 factory default A4. Must match the radio CI-V menu.

data.name: Settings.Radio.Ic706CivHint
data.value: IC-706 factory default 48H. Must match the radio CI-V address (Initial Set Mode).

data.name: Settings.Radio.Ic706MkiiCivHint
data.value: IC-706MKII factory default 4CH. Must match the radio CI-V address.

data.name: Settings.Radio.Ic706MkiiGCivHint
data.value: IC-706MKIIG factory default 58H. Must match menu 34 CI-V ADDRES.

data.name: Settings.Radio.Ft817Hint
data.value: FT-817 / FT-818: Set menu #14 to 4800 baud on each radio (38400 also works). 8N2 CAT — one COM per leg. Linear: spin downlink for passband; FM locks dial while tracking.

data.name: Settings.Radio.Ic705Hint
data.value: IC-705: Set Connectors → CI-V → CI-V USB Port to Link to [CI-V] (not REMOTE). Match CI-V baud in Settings (default 115200). One COM port per leg — use the port labelled CI-V when two appear.

data.name: Settings.Radio.Ic706Hint
data.value: IC-706: CI-V via the REMOTE jack (or CT-17). 2m only (no 70cm). Set CI-V address and baud in Initial Set Mode (LOCK at power-on) to match Settings (default 48H / 19200). CI-V TRN On recommended.

data.name: Settings.Radio.Ic706MkiiHint
data.value: IC-706MKII: same CI-V setup as IC-706. 2m only (no 70cm). Factory default address 4CH.

data.name: Settings.Radio.Ic706MkiiGHint
data.value: IC-706MKIIG: CI-V via the REMOTE jack (or CT-17). Set menu 34 CI-V ADDRES to match Settings (default 58H), menu 35 CI-V BAUD to match Settings (default 19200), and menu 36 CI-V TRN On. Covers 2m and 70cm; 23cm satellites are not supported.

data.name: Settings.Radio.Ft991Hint
data.value: FT-991 / FT-991A: Match menu 031 CAT RATE to Settings (default 38400). 8N2 ASCII CAT with hardware RTS — use the USB CAT COM port. One COM per leg. FM locks the VFO-A dial while tracking.

data.name: Settings.Radio.Ftx1Hint
data.value: FTX-1 Field / FTX-1optima: Match menu CAT-1 RATE to Settings (default 38400). 8N2 ASCII CAT with hardware RTS — use the Enhanced COM port (CAT-1), not CAT-2. One COM per leg. FM locks the MAIN VFO dial while tracking.

data.name: Settings.Radio.CwSideband
data.value: Linear CW: keep receive in USB/LSB (transmit CW only)

data.name: Settings.Radio.CwSidebandNote
data.value: Applies when the frequency panel is set to CW on linear SSB satellites. Checked is the usual split (CW TX, USB/LSB RX). Unchecked sets both VFOs to CW.

data.name: Settings.Radio.DopplerFmNote
data.value: FM / ISS cross-band: lower = more CAT updates (default 350 Hz).

data.name: Settings.Radio.DopplerSsbNote
data.value: Frequencies are sent to the radio only when drift exceeds this interval (SatPC32-style).

data.name: Settings.Radio.DopplerCatLeadNote
data.value: When enabled, CAT and the Radio row predict Doppler ahead on the steep TCA leg only (when range rate is changing quickly). Lead is half your CAT delay, capped at 50 ms. Gentle AOS/LOS legs use the current rate. The Sat row still shows the current spacecraft frequency.

data.name: Settings.Rotator.TypeNote
data.value: GS-232 for Yaesu and clones. SPID for controllers in SP mode (Rot1Prog / Rot2Prog). EasyComm for EC mode — SPID, M2, and other text-protocol controllers.

data.name: Settings.Rotator.SmartAzimuthNote
data.value: On 450° rotators (e.g. Yaesu G-5500), commands 361–450° avoid a long slew back through 0° when tracking passes over north.

data.name: Settings.Rotator.TrackStartNote
data.value: Default is -3° (below horizon). Range -90° to 90°. The rotator begins slewing when the tracked satellite rises above this elevation, and parks once it drops below.

data.name: Settings.Rotator.ParkNote
data.value: Park position is used when no satellite is above the tracking threshold. The focused pass or live satellite is tracked automatically.

data.name: Settings.Rotator.OffsetNote
data.value: Added to pass tracking and manual rotate commands (not park). Use positive values if the beam stops short of the target; negative if it overshoots.

data.name: Settings.Recording.EnableNote
data.value: Records WAV audio from the selected input device while the focused satellite is above the start elevation. Files are saved with UTC timestamps.

data.name: Settings.Recording.StopNote
data.value: Stop elevation should be at or below the start elevation to avoid flapping near the threshold.

data.name: Settings.Recording.Unavailable
data.value: Audio recording is not available on this system.

data.name: Settings.HamsAt.Intro
data.value: Show upcoming satellite roves from hams.at in the sidebar. Only alerts marked workable for your hams.at location are listed — set your location on hams.at if none appear. Your API key is stored locally in settings.json like other integrations.

data.name: Settings.HamsAt.EnterApiKey
data.value: Enter your hams.at API key first.

data.name: Settings.Cloudlog.Intro
data.value: Posts satellite TX/RX to Cloudlog when frequencies or modes change, plus a periodic keepalive while unchanged. Test connection loads your logbooks for grid lookups on hams.at roves.

data.name: Settings.Cloudlog.Enable
data.value: Enable Cloudlog radio API (required for live updates)

data.name: Settings.Cloudlog.RadioNameNote
data.value: Default is OscarWatch. Must match a radio entry Cloudlog can assign QSOs to.

data.name: Settings.Cloudlog.MinIntervalNote
data.value: Posts immediately when uplink, downlink, modes, or satellite change. Repeats the same state at this interval (default 10 minutes) so Cloudlog stays in sync during long contacts.

data.name: Settings.Cloudlog.EnterCredentials
data.value: Enter URL and API key (tab out of each field or click Test again).

data.name: Settings.Cloudlog.CheckRoveGrids
data.value: Highlight grids I still need on hams.at roves

data.name: Settings.Cloudlog.ConnectionOk
data.value: Connection OK — posted test CAT to {0}

data.name: Pass.CountMutual
data.value: {0} mutual pass(es) in the next {1} h

data.name: Planner.FiltersNote
data.value: Filters apply to this list and the main window when you close.

data.name: Planner.Export.CalendarTip
data.value: Export all passes for this satellite to an iCalendar (.ics) file

data.name: Mutual.Description
data.value: Find passes when the satellite is above minimum elevation at both your station and the remote grid square at the same time.

data.name: Mutual.Status.EnterGrid
data.value: Enter the remote operator's Maidenhead grid square (at least 4 characters).

data.name: Mutual.Status.NoPasses
data.value: No mutual passes in the next {0} h for {1} and {2}.

data.name: Mutual.Visualizer.DoubleClickHint
data.value: Double-click a pass to open the mutual pass visualiser. Right-click for more options.

data.name: Mutual.Visualizer.StationStats
data.value: {0} · AOS Az: {1:F1}° · Max El: {2:F1}° · LOS Az: {3:F1}°

data.name: Sunlight.Status.SelectPredict
data.value: Select a satellite and click Predict.

data.name: Sunlight.Status.NoSatellites
data.value: No enabled satellites — use Satellites → Select Satellites first.

data.name: DbEditor.Intro
data.value: Manage satellite transponder modes (frequencies in kHz). Saved to your user AppData folder.

data.name: DbEditor.ModeHint
data.value: Set uplink to 0 for beacon-only. Leave CTCSS at 0 for none. FM modes use Hz (e.g. 67.0).

data.name: DbEditor.Status.AlreadyExists
data.value: Satellite “{0}” is already in the database.

data.name: DbEditor.Status.ImportNoChanges
data.value: Import file matches the editor (no new or changed entries).

data.name: DbEditor.Status.ImportApplied
data.value: Import applied to editor — press Save to write to your user database.

data.name: DbEditor.Status.UpToDate
data.value: Transponder database is up to date.

data.name: DbMerge.Intro.Import
data.value: New entries are selected by default. Conflicts keep your editor copy unless you choose the imported version.

data.name: DbMerge.Intro.Remote
data.value: New entries are selected by default. Conflicts keep your local copy unless you choose the published version.

data.name: DbAdd.Intro.WithCatalog
data.value: Choose a satellite from your TLE catalog, or enter a custom name below.

data.name: DbAdd.Intro.NoCatalog
data.value: Every TLE satellite is already in this database. Enter a custom name below.

data.name: DbAdd.CustomNameLabel
data.value: Custom name (optional if you selected above)

data.name: DbAdd.CustomNameWatermark
data.value: Type name if not in list…

data.name: DbAdd.Error.PickOne
data.value: Select a TLE satellite or enter a custom name.

data.name: Update.Available.Message
data.value:
A newer version of OscarWatch is available.
You are running {0}. Latest: {1} ({2})
